front: What is the primary focus of Module 5 in an Introduction to Data Structures course?
back: Module 5 typically covers non-linear data structures, focusing on Trees and Graphs, including their properties, traversal algorithms, and common applications.

front: Name two common types of tree data structures studied in this module.
back: Binary Trees (including Binary Search Trees) and Heaps (Min-Heap, Max-Heap) are fundamental tree structures covered.

front: What are the two main traversal methods for trees?
back: Depth-First Search (DFS) - e.g., In-order, Pre-order, Post-order - and Breadth-First Search (BFS), also known as level-order traversal.

front: What key concept differentiates a graph from a tree?
back: Graphs can contain cycles and do not necessarily have a hierarchical parent-child relationship or a single root node, unlike trees which are acyclic and hierarchical.

front: What is a common real-world application of graph data structures?
back: Social networks use graphs to represent users (vertices) and their connections (edges); navigation apps like Google Maps use graphs to find the shortest path between locations.
